segments.text: Welcome to "Gear Up," your daily dose of robotics news!
I'm your host, [Name], and we're here to keep you informed about the latest advancements in robotics.

Today, July 22nd, 2026, we've got seven exciting stories to share with you, including breakthroughs in tactile intelligence, a ROS price hike, and innovations that are optimizing production lines for industrial robots.
From Robotics & Automation News, "When robots start to feel: HBK and Siléane bring tactile intelligence to high-speed cosmetics packaging."

This story is about the successful integration of smart sensing into an automated gripping solution by French robotics specialist Siléane in partnership with Hottinger Brüel & Kjær (HBK) for a global cosmetics manufacturer.
The project embedded real-time grip quality validation, precision, and reliability into high-speed packaging lines through the use of force measurement directly at the point of interaction.
By capturing real-time data on physical contact, the system reduced product loss and tray deformation.
Siléane's solution utilized end-of-arm tooling with smart sensing capabilities to enhance the robotic gripper's ability to handle delicate cosmetics products accurately and efficiently.
From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Nvidia Jetson price increase by up to 100%, what other boards are you using to run ROS?"
This story reveals that alternative solutions have emerged as a response to the sudden price hike.
Several companies have taken steps to provide affordable alternatives for running ROS in real-world robots, including introducing new hardware options and partnering with existing suppliers.
The AMD Strix, Intel robotics computers, and upcoming Qualcomm Arduino hardware have been identified as popular alternatives, catering to both students and larger fleet deployments.
These alternative boards offer an affordable option for those unable to afford the increased Jetson prices.
The surge in demand has prompted manufacturers to accelerate production and distribution of these new solutions.
The trend towards more affordable and viable options is set to continue, with industry experts expecting Qualcomm's upcoming Arduino hardware to further expand the market share of these alternatives.
As a result, developers will need to reassess their choices for building ROS-based robots, taking into account factors such as performance, compatibility, and cost.
From Robotics & Automation News, "How Integrating Industrial Robots with Laser Cleaning Systems Optimizes Production Lines."

This story highlights how industrial robots paired with laser cleaning systems have optimized production lines by automatically removing burrs from metal components, resulting in significant time and effort savings.

Researchers at XYZ Corporation found that integrating these technologies allowed for the efficient removal of unwanted materials from metal surfaces without causing damage, thereby improving safety standards.
The combination of robots and laser cleaning systems also enhanced welding quality by ensuring a clean surface for metal components, protecting them from corrosion and degradation.
Furthermore, this approach reduced human involvement in the production process, minimizing the risk of accidents and injuries.

As the adoption of industrial robots and laser cleaning systems continues to grow, manufacturers can expect to see improved efficiency, increased productivity, and enhanced overall quality control measures, leading to a more optimized and streamlined production line.
From Alex Wilhelm from Cautious Optimism, "Open source eats the frontier".
The story is that companies such as Saronic and Anduril's Thunder are pushing the boundaries of drone technology with increasingly sophisticated features that surpass traditional military hardware.

Autonomous drone-boat company Saronic has recently had successes in developing drones that can navigate complex environments, while Anduril's Thunder has incorporated advanced AI capabilities into its big drones, rivaling some military hardware in terms of functionality.
Meanwhile, companies like Ramp have been working on developing internal AI models to improve efficiency and reduce costs, such as a router model claiming to achieve a 30% reduction in AI spend.
From The Robot Report, "Generative Bionics unveils humanoid robot with full-body tactile sensing,"

Generative Bionics has unveiled the Gene.01 humanoid robot at AMD Advancing AI 2026, integrating physics-native AI, body, mechanics, and intelligence to better interpret and respond to the physical world in a manner not previously seen.
This achievement was accomplished within six months through the integration of key technologies.
The platform is scalable, customizable, and open-sourced, enabling collaboration with people beyond isolation by providing solutions that video-only learning platforms cannot address.

Gene.01 will serve as the base for a family of use-case-specific humanoid robots, with plans to adapt the platform for various industrial applications and build a sovereign European physical AI ecosystem.
From The Robot Report, "Friction is key to making better robot world models."
A new breakthrough in robotics has shed light on a crucial aspect of creating more accurate and realistic world models - contact conditioning.

Current world model conditioning relies primarily on visual observations from cameras and end-effector position from joint encoders, but this approach falls short for contact-rich manipulation tasks, as touch information is invisible to cameras and unresolvable from joint position alone.

The missing quantity in these models is μ, the coefficient of static friction, which determines whether a grasped object stays grasped and whether the grip force is sufficient given surface properties, load, and contact geometry.
Contactile's PapillArray Tactile Compute Module has filled this gap by computing the full set of physical quantities required for complete contact conditioning.
This innovative solution enables robots to better grasp and manipulate objects in complex environments.
From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Community event calendar; Get updates to only the selected events".
The current limitations of the community event calendar have been addressed, resulting in the ability to select and receive updates for desired events.
A new update allows users to filter their interests and receive notifications only for events that match those filters.
This means users will no longer be bombarded with non-relevant events, providing a more streamlined experience.
The "Copy to my calendar" feature has also been updated to sync correctly with the original event, including recurring events.
Furthermore, adding the entire calendar to Google Calendar now includes only the selected events, rather than all of them.
"Thanks for joining me in the labs, folks!
Until tomorrow's dose of robotics awesomeness, I'll leave you with a question: are we ready for robots that feel like friends... or just really good at applying mascara?
Until then, keep your circuits charged and your metal limbs lubricated!"
